reasoning: The document shows moderate fidelity (0.72) with significant visual and content defects concentrated on the final page. While programmatic shingling recall is high, the elevated duplication ratio (0.108) corroborates the LLM's report of duplicated logos, and the specific omission of contact handles and department names represents a meaningful loss of utility for the 'Let's Connect!' section.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The final section (Page 15, 'Let's Connect!') is missing a substantial amount of text content. While the title and the URL 'it.nc.gov' are present, the social media handles (@NCDIT, @BroadbandIO, @ncicenter), the account name (NCDIT), and the full department names ('NC Department of Information Technology', 'NC DIT') are missing from the text elements. These represent the majority of the information on that page.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document on page 15 contains text: '@NCDIT', '@BroadbandIO', '@ncicenter', 'NCDIT', 'NC Department of Information Technology', and 'NC DIT'.
concerns.output_quote: The final section of the JSON output only contains the text 'Let's Connect!' and 'it.nc.gov'. The social handles and department names are omitted.

concerns.analysis: The source PDF on page 15 includes five meaningful social media brand icons (Twitter, YouTube, LinkedIn, Flickr, and Facebook) which serve to identify the platforms for the provided handles. These icons are entirely missing from the HTML output; only the associated text strings are rendered.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 15 of the PDF contains visual icons for Twitter, YouTube, LinkedIn, Facebook, and Flickr platforms.
concerns.output_quote: The 'Let's Connect!' section in the HTML lacks these social media brand icons, showing only the text handles.
concerns.location: Final section 'Let's Connect!', corresponding to PDF page 15

concerns.analysis: The 'NC DIT' logo image is visually duplicated multiple times in the final section of the converted document. While page 15 of the source PDF contains only one instance of this logo, the HTML output displays it three times within the 'Let's Connect!' section.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF page 15 shows a single 'NC DIT' logo in the bottom right corner.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML section for 'Let's Connect!' contains three separate renderings of the 'NC DIT' logo intermixed with or following the text content.
concerns.location: Final section 'Let's Connect!', corresponding to PDF page 15

Resolve the duplication of the NC DIT logo in the footer/contact section
